title: lappula redowskii var. cupulata
intro: Lappula redowskii var. cupulata, also known as the Russian bugloss or small bugloss, is a perennial plant species within the family Boraginaceae, native to Russia and eastern Asia. Traditionally, it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lappula redowskii var. cupulata include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
